refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper decomposition and allocation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) via on-board sensors and/or site survey. Compare against productivity_target. Document operator mode (on-site or remote), task type, and any safety incidents or policy violations. Pass if measured productivity ≥ target AND zero safety violations.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and repeatability to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, operational policies) during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) in real-time and post-operation for verification against productivity_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic/electric drivetrain with precision bucket control and autonomous task sequencing to maximize cycle time and material handling rate.
DP-B: Provide dual-mode operator interface (on-site cab with direct controls + remote teleoperation with video/sensor feedback) to enable flexible deployment and continuous operation.
DP-C: Implement real-time safety monitoring (GNSS geofencing, collision avoidance, load limits) with automatic task interruption on policy violation.
DP-D: Deploy on-board telemetry system (sensors, data logger, wireless uplink) to capture and transmit productivity metrics (cycle time, material volume, timestamps) to cloud backend for analysis.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (high-performance vehicle actuation), DP-B (dual-mode operator interface), DP-C (real-time safety enforcement), and DP-D (telemetry & metrics) as an integrated design solution. Each DP addresses a distinct sub-FR; together they enable the parent FR (productivity KPI achievement).

potential_sources: DP-A and DP-B are coupled via operator control latency: remote teleoperation introduces network delay that may degrade precision control required for high-cycle-rate tasks. Mitigation: predictive control algorithms and local autonomy.

DP-C and DP-A are coupled: safety constraints (e.g., load limits, geofencing) may reduce achievable cycle rate. Mitigation: design safety margins to allow near-target productivity without frequent interruptions.
DP-D and DP-B are coupled: telemetry uplink competes with teleoperation command/video bandwidth. Mitigation: prioritize teleoperation traffic; batch telemetry uploads during idle periods.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via design trade-offs and architectural prioritization. No fundamental contradiction detected. Recommend explicit bandwidth allocation and latency budgets in detailed design.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic/electric technology and operator skill level. Risk: If target is unrealistic, no DP will satisfy FR. Mitigation: validate target against historical data and vendor benchmarks.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and stable. Risk: Ambiguous or changing policies will cause DP-C to fail. Mitigation: require formal safety plan document as input to design.

Assumption: Network connectivity (for teleoperation and telemetry) is reliable (>95% uptime). Risk: Intermittent connectivity will degrade remote operation and metrics reporting. Mitigation: design local fallback (on-site operation) and offline data buffering.

Assumption: Operator training and certification are adequate. Risk: Untrained operators will not achieve productivity_target regardless of DP quality. Mitigation: include operator training and competency assessment in acceptance criteria.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-Integrated into explicit sub-DPs aligned with sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 → DP-A, FR-NGHE-001.2 → DP-B, etc.) to enable clear allocation to implementation modules and reduce ambiguity in design reviews.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary encompassing all hardware (vehicle, sensors, actuators, network equipment) and software (control logic, teleoperation interface, safety monitor, telemetry backend) required to achieve the productivity KPI. It is the integration point for all sub-systems and the primary ownership boundary for the System Engineer and Project Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Input: Site Manager or Operator provides task definition (dig location, load target, haul destination, material type). Format: structured task command (coordinates, volume target, priority). Frequency: per-task (typically 1–10 tasks/shift).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Output: NGHE Solution transmits productivity metrics (cycle time, material volume, timestamps, operator mode, safety events) to backend analytics system. Format: time-series sensor data + aggregated KPIs. Frequency: real-time (1 Hz) + post-operation summary.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Output: NGHE Solution broadcasts safety alerts (geofence breach, collision warning, load limit exceeded, policy violation) to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. Format: alert message with severity, location, timestamp. Frequency: event-driven (immediate on violation).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional: NGHE Solution communicates with remote Tele-operator (command/video) and backend systems (telemetry, safety alerts, software updates). Format: IP-based (4G/5G or satellite). Frequency: continuous (teleoperation) + periodic (telemetry, updates).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. All sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained within it. Diffusion risk is minimal at this level; risk increases at sub-system decomposition. Ownership: System Engineer (primary), Project Manager (oversight).

Vehicle module contains the physical platform (chassis, drivetrain, bucket, sensors). It directly realizes DP-A (high-performance actuation). However, Vehicle does not own teleoperation interface (DP-B), safety enforcement logic (DP-C), or telemetry backend (DP-D). These are diffused across Teleoperation and Safety Services modules. Ownership: Equipment OEM or Vehicle Systems Engineer.

Teleoperation module contains remote operator interface, video/sensor feedback, and command transmission (DP-B). However, it depends on Vehicle for actuation and Network Link for connectivity. Safety enforcement (DP-C) is partially in Teleoperation (remote monitoring) but primarily in Safety Services. Telemetry (DP-D) is diffused to backend. Ownership: Teleoperation Systems Engineer or Software Architect.

Safety Services module contains real-time safety monitoring, geofencing, collision avoidance, and policy enforcement (DP-C). It also owns SafetyAlert interface. However, it depends on Vehicle sensors and Teleoperation for operator context. Telemetry (DP-D) is partially in Safety Services (safety event logging) but primarily in backend. Ownership: Safety Manager or Safety Systems Engineer.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System-level ownership by System Engineer and Project Manager, with sub-ownership delegated to Vehicle OEM, Teleoperation vendor, and Safety Services provider).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001 because it encompasses the entire integrated system required to achieve the productivity KPI. The four sub-DPs (DP-A through DP-D) are distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, but their integration and coordination are owned at the NGHE Solution level. This allows the System Engineer to manage cross-module dependencies (e.g., latency budgets, bandwidth allocation, safety-productivity trade-offs) and ensures accountability for the end-to-end KPI.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is too broad; it may obscure accountability for specific sub-systems. Mitigation: Decompose into Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services at the next level, with explicit interface contracts and ownership assignments.

Risk: Teleoperation and Safety Services modules are external (vendor-supplied); integration and testing complexity is high. Mitigation: Define detailed interface specifications (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and conduct integration testing early.

Risk: Network Link is a critical dependency; failure or latency will degrade both teleoperation (DP-B) and telemetry (DP-D). Mitigation: Design redundancy (dual network paths) and local fallback (on-site operation without remote link).

Risk: Productivity_target may conflict with safety constraints (DP-C); aggressive cycle rates may trigger frequent safety interruptions. Mitigation: Conduct trade-off analysis and validate that target is achievable within safety margins.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: At the next decomposition level, explicitly allocate sub-DPs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules with clear interface contracts. Define performance budgets (latency, bandwidth, safety margin) to manage cross-module coupling. Establish integration test plan to verify that sub-systems work together to achieve the productivity KPI.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (productivity KPI achievement with safety compliance) is directly addressed by the selected DP (integrated solution combining high-performance actuation, dual-mode operator interface, real-time safety enforcement, and telemetry). The DP is logically sound and feasible with current technology. No fundamental contradiction or infeasibility detected. Coupling risks are manageable via design trade-offs.

diffusion_risks: DP-A (vehicle actuation) is primarily contained in Vehicle module, but control logic may be diffused across Vehicle and Teleoperation (for remote operation). Mitigation: define clear control architecture (e.g., Vehicle owns low-level servo control; Teleoperation owns high-level task sequencing).
DP-B (operator interface) is split between on-site cab (Vehicle) and remote teleoperation (Teleoperation module). Mitigation: ensure consistent command semantics and feedback across both modes.

DP-C (safety enforcement) is distributed across Vehicle (sensor integration), Safety Services (policy logic), and Teleoperation (operator monitoring). Mitigation: define safety-critical interfaces and implement redundancy.

DP-D (telemetry) is diffused across Vehicle (sensor data collection), Safety Services (event logging), and backend (analytics). Mitigation: standardize data format and ensure end-to-end data integrity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the integration and coordination of all sub-DPs. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules with clear ownership and interfaces. The System Engineer (NGHE Solution owner) is responsible for managing cross-module dependencies and ensuring that the integrated system achieves the productivity KPI.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial (8-hour shift, 5-day week) with measurement of actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) and safety compliance. Telemetry interface (Tasking input, Telemetry output, SafetyAlert output) provides data for post-operation analysis. Acceptance criteria are clear and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.3), and backend telemetry (FR-NGHE-001.4) are defined and will be refined at the next decomposition level. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Refine sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) into detailed requirements with acceptance criteria and verification methods.
Define performance budgets (latency, bandwidth, safety margin) to manage cross-module coupling and ensure that integrated system achieves productivity_target.
Establish interface control documents (ICDs) for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link to ensure consistent integration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Conduct trade-off analysis to validate that productivity_target is achievable within safety constraints and network reliability assumptions.
Define operator training and competency assessment as part of acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) to enable clear allocation to implementation modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass the gate review, but further decomposition is necessary to define detailed requirements, performance budgets, and interface contracts. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership, reduce ambiguity, and enable detailed design and verification.
decision.justification_detail: The NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate for the current level, but it is too broad to guide detailed design. Decomposition into Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules will enable allocation of sub-DPs and sub-FRs to specific teams and vendors. This will reduce integration risk and improve accountability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and repeatability to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints (worksite plan, operational policies) during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) in real-time and post-operation for verification against productivity_target.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic/electric technology and operator skill level.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and stable.
Network connectivity (for teleoperation and telemetry) is reliable (>95% uptime).
Operator training and certification are adequate.
Representative operations trial (8-hour shift, 5-day week) is a valid verification method.
On-board sensors and/or site survey can accurately measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h).
open_risks: Productivity_target may be unrealistic; validation against historical data and vendor benchmarks is needed.
Network connectivity failures will degrade teleoperation and telemetry; redundancy and fallback mechanisms are required.
Safety constraints may conflict with productivity target; trade-off analysis is needed to validate feasibility.
Operator skill and training are critical; inadequate training will prevent achievement of productivity_target.
Integration complexity across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules is high; early integration testing is essential.
Latency and bandwidth constraints for teleoperation and telemetry are not yet quantified; performance budgets must be defined.
evidence_needed: Historical productivity data from similar equipment and operations to validate productivity_target.
Formal worksite safety plan and operational policies document.
Network connectivity analysis (coverage, latency, bandwidth) for teleoperation and telemetry.
Operator training and competency assessment plan.
Detailed design specifications for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Interface control documents (ICDs) for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Performance budgets (latency, bandwidth, safety margin) for cross-module integration.
Integration test plan and acceptance criteria for representative operations trial.
